vocab.meaning: trông vô cùng lộng lẫy/cuốn hút
vocab.example: I sometimes carry a pocket mirror to ensure I look a million dollars before walking into a gala or a wedding.
I keep a small mirror in my glove box just in case I need to spruce myself up after a long commute.

vocab.meaning: ngay lập tức khi đang làm việc khác/đang vội
vocab.example: Having a mirror in my bag allows me to fix my hair on the fly between back-to-back meetings.

vocab.meaning: còn chút luộm thuộm/chưa hoàn hảo
vocab.example: I don't carry a mirror, so I often end up looking a bit rough around the edges by the end of a workday.

vocab.meaning: ăn mặc cực kỳ sang trọng/chỉnh tề
vocab.example: Whenever I'm dressed to the nines, a portable mirror becomes an essential item to maintain that perfect look.

vocab.meaning: cực kỳ giống với ai đó
vocab.example: Sometimes looking in my travel mirror reminds me that I bear a striking resemblance to my grandfather.

vocab.meaning: tạo ấn tượng tốt nhất có thể
vocab.example: I use a mirror to check my appearance because I always want to put my best foot forward when meeting new clients.

vocab.meaning: người bạn chỉ ở bên khi thuận lợi (nghĩa bóng: vật dụng chỉ dùng khi cần)
vocab.example: My compact mirror is a bit of a fair-weather friend; I only remember to take it when I'm wearing heavy makeup.
I quickly glance at my mirror to make sure my face is as clean as a whistle after having a messy lunch.

vocab.meaning: không lo lắng/bận tâm về điều gì
vocab.example: If I forget to bring a mirror, I don't lose any sleep over it; I'm not that vain about my hair.
